hook.scenario: Blinkit shows you a '20% off' tag, but they marked up the base price by 25% first.
hook.question: Are you really getting a discount, or just paying the original price?

mental_model.intuition: Cost Price (CP) is the anchor. Everything scales relative to what the seller paid.
mental_model.description: Profit and loss are percentage increases and decreases calculated on the Cost Price. Discount is a percentage decrease calculated on the Marked Price.

pattern_discovery.setup: Compare the profit or loss against the original cost.
pattern_discovery.prompt: What is the constant denominator used for calculating profit or loss percentage?
pattern_discovery.rule: Cost Price (CP) is the anchor. Always calculate P/L percentage relative to what was paid originally.

formula.explain: Never calculate Profit or Loss on Selling Price unless explicitly asked. Always calculate Discount on Marked Price (MP).

shortcuts.title: Multiplier Method
shortcuts.trick: For 20% profit, SP = 1.2 × CP. For 15% loss, SP = 0.85 × CP. This skips the intermediate add/subtract step.
shortcuts.example: If CP is 400 and profit is 25%, SP = 400 × 1.25 = 500.

shortcuts.title: Successive Discount
shortcuts.trick: Two successive discounts of a% and b% equal a single discount of (a + b - ab/100)%.
shortcuts.example: Successive discounts of 20% and 10% = 20 + 10 - (20×10/100) = 28% total discount, not 30%.

common_mistakes.title: Base Confusion
common_mistakes.explanation: Calculating profit percentage on Selling Price instead of Cost Price. 20 profit on 120 SP is a 16.66% profit, not 20%.

common_mistakes.title: Fake Net Zero
common_mistakes.explanation: Thinking a 20% markup followed by a 20% discount brings you back to the original price. (1.2 × 0.8 = 0.96, which is a 4% loss).

recall_questions.q: If an item is bought for ₹500 and sold for ₹600, what is the profit percentage?
A shirt marked at ₹1000 is sold after a 15% discount. What is the selling price?
If SP is ₹900 after a 10% loss, what was the CP?

oa_questions.question: A shopkeeper marks his goods 25% above CP and allows a discount of 10%. Find his overall profit percentage.
By selling 33 meters of cloth, a person gains the selling price of 11 meters. Find his gain percentage.
